edge_hypothesis: MACD tail harvesters achieved PF=1.71 (7:1) and PF=1.36 (5:1) on 1h — both ACCEPTs. But the transitional regime still drags PF down (transitional PF=1.31 vs ranging PF=2.06). This spec tests a COMPLEMENTARY approach: time-bounded momentum capture. The thesis is that MACD histogram zero-crosses that don't produce immediate directional movement (within 8-15 bars on 1h) are failed signals trapped in the transitional regime. By auto-exiting stale trades via strict max_bars_in_trade, we cut losses in transitional periods while preserving winners in ranging/trending. Also tests 4h timeframe to compare against the 1h tail harvester success.
invalidation: If time-capped variants show lower PF than the uncapped tail harvesters (PF=1.71), time-bounding hurts more than it helps — stick with pure tail harvesting. If 4h variant underperforms 1h, confirms that 1h is the optimal MACD timeframe.

variants.name: macd_time_capped_12bar_moderate_rr
variants.description: Time-bounded MACD: 12-bar max hold with moderate 4:1 R:R. Hypothesis: MACD zero-crosses that haven't reached TP within 12 bars (~12h on 1h chart) are failed signals in choppy conditions. The 12-bar time cap acts as a proxy transitional-regime filter — winners in trending/ranging regimes move fast, losers in transitional stagnate. Moderate R:R (4:1) is achievable within the time window.
entry_long: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses from <= 0 to > 0 (histogram zero-cross, bullish momentum shift)
entry_short: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses from >= 0 to < 0 (histogram zero-cross, bearish momentum shift)
exit_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR from entry
Exit after 12 bars if neither TP nor stop hit (time cap — cut stale trades)
Exit on opposite MACD histogram zero-cross
risk_rules: TP: 4.0x ATR (2.67:1 R:R — achievable within 12-bar window)
Time cap at 12 bars acts as implicit transitional-regime filter

components.notes: MACDh_12_26_9 histogram zero-cross for momentum shift

variants.name: macd_time_capped_8bar_tight
variants.description: Aggressive time-bounded MACD: 8-bar max hold with tighter 3:1 R:R. Tests whether extremely short holding periods concentrate returns in the initial momentum burst following a MACD zero-cross. On 1h chart, 8 bars = 8 hours. If MACD momentum is real, 8h should be enough for a 3x ATR move. If not, the signal was noise.
exit_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR from entry
Exit after 8 bars if neither TP nor stop hit (aggressive time cap)
Exit on opposite MACD histogram zero-cross
risk_rules: 8-bar aggressive time cap — tests immediate momentum hypothesis

components.notes: MACDh_12_26_9 histogram zero-cross for momentum shift

variants.name: macd_4h_moderate_tail_5to1
variants.description: 4h timeframe comparison: MACD tail harvester on 4h with moderate 5:1 R:R. The original MACD ACCEPT (macd_wide_exit_fee_killer) was on 4h at PF=1.347 with 189 trades. Recent 1h tail harvesters achieved PF=1.71. This variant tests whether the 4h tail-harvester concept with wider stops (2.0x vs original 1.0x) improves the already-accepted 4h result. The wider stop should reduce DD below the original 6.2%.
exit_rules: TP at 5.0x ATR from entry (moderate tail harvester for 4h)
Stop loss at 2.0x ATR from entry (wider than original 1.0x ACCEPT)
Exit on opposite MACD histogram zero-cross
risk_rules: Stop: 2.0x ATR (wider than original ACCEPT at 1.0x — targeting DD reduction)
4h timeframe — fewer signals but each captures multi-day trend moves

components.notes: MACDh_12_26_9 histogram zero-cross for momentum shift
ATR_14 for stop/TP scaling, wider stop than original ACCEPT
